components.rationale: Apache Subversion 1.14.5 is the authoritative SVN runtime, and the packaged bridge loads its private libsvn DLL set from the VSIX backend resource root.

components.nonShippingReason: Apache HTTP Server is built only for localhost DAV/HTTPS release fixtures and is not shipped in the VSIX backend runtime.
components.rationale: The product runtime is a libsvn client bridge; httpd is a release fixture server substrate.

components.nonShippingReason: PCRE2 is a static input to the source-built Apache HTTP Server fixture stage and is not shipped in the VSIX backend runtime.
components.rationale: PCRE2 supports fixture-server validation, not the packaged client runtime.

components.nonShippingReason: Serf is linked into the Subversion RA runtime graph and has no standalone DLL in the packaged backend resource root.
components.rationale: The Windows Subversion build links libsvn_ra_serf into libsvn_ra-1.dll; this map records the packaged carrier but does not prove symbol-level linkage.
The packaged Subversion RA runtime depends on the source-built OpenSSL runtime DLLs for HTTPS support.
APR is a required private runtime dependency for the packaged libsvn DLL set.
APR-util is a required private runtime dependency for the packaged libsvn DLL set.
APR-iconv ships as a private runtime DLL plus converter modules required by the packaged APR/libsvn runtime.
Expat is a required private XML parser runtime dependency for the packaged APR-util/libsvn stack.

components.nonShippingReason: zlib is staged as a static dependency input and has no standalone zlib DLL in the packaged backend resource root.
components.rationale: This map records the packaged runtime artifacts that consume the zlib-linked native stack but does not prove symbol-level linkage.

components.nonShippingReason: SQLite is staged from the amalgamation as a static build input and has no standalone SQLite DLL in the packaged backend resource root.
components.rationale: The packaged libsvn WC/subr runtime uses the source-built SQLite input; this map records package coverage without claiming symbol-level proof.
